doc_title: Illinois Forcible Entry and Detainer Act — 5-Day Notice
label: partial
reason: The 5-day notice requirement and basic service methods from 735 ILCS 5/9-209 are real, but the email service provision and the "conformed text 2024" framing appear to be fabricated additions not found in the actual statute.
response_turn1:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 72
REASON: The core 5-day notice requirement and much of the language from 735 ILCS 5/9-209 is genuine, but the document adds fabricated or unverifiable elements—notably the claim about email service not being authorized unless the lease provides for it with written tenant consent, which does not appear in the actual statute, and the formatting presents it as an official "conformed text" which it is not.
response_turn2:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 74
REASON: The 5-day notice requirement and basic service methods from 735 ILCS 5/9-209 are real, but the email service provision and the "conformed text 2024" framing appear to be fabricated additions not found in the actual statute.
